title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
audience: Readers who understand dot-product attention and want the paper's formulation, implementation, and empirical findings.
central_question: Can absolute position be encoded in query and key representations while their inner product depends explicitly on relative position?

media.caption: Five-second Manim scene for the Eq. 16 collapse from separate query/key rotations to n minus m.
English captions for the relative-rotation micro-video.
New Manim scene connecting the paper's stated properties to Table 1 and Figure 3 findings.
Poster for the RoPE findings scene.
English captions for the RoPE findings scene.

paper_excerpts.text: The paper reviews additive absolute and relative position encodings and motivates a multiplicative construction with sequence length flexibility, long-term decay, and compatibility with linear self-attention.
Equation 11 formulates the relative-position requirement. Equations 14-16 give the rotary solution and show that the query-key inner product contains R at relative position n minus m.

Table 1 reports 27.5 BLEU for RoFormer and 27.3 for Transformer-base on WMT 2014 English-to-German. Figure 3 reports faster convergence in the shown language-model pre-training and Performer experiments.

code_notes.text: The official pseudocode constructs the perpendicular companion, applies q cos plus q-perpendicular sin and the analogous key operation, then takes the standard attention dot product.

agent_input.claim_policy: Use the paper's terms: formulation of relative position encoding, rotary position embedding, sequence length flexibility, long-term decay, and linear self-attention. Keep exact algebra separate from empirical findings and extrapolation claims.
